prerequisites: A MailerLite account with access to the dashboard.
An API token generated from the MailerLite dashboard (Integrations > MailerLite API), supplied as the required api_token config field.

authMethods.name: API token (Bearer)
authMethods.summary: MailerLite authenticates with an API token sent as an HTTP bearer credential (Authorization: Bearer <api_token>). Provide this value in the connector's api_token field. Requests also require Content-Type: application/json and Accept: application/json headers against the base URL https://connect.mailerlite.com/api.

setupSteps.title: Generate a MailerLite API token
setupSteps.body: Sign in to the MailerLite dashboard and open Integrations from the sidebar. In the MailerLite API section click Use, then Generate new token. Give the token a descriptive name, optionally set IP restrictions, and click Create token. Copy the token immediately and store it securely, because you cannot view it again after creation.

setupSteps.title: Provide the token to the connector
setupSteps.body: Paste the generated token into the connector's required api_token field. This is the only required configuration value. The connector sends it as Authorization: Bearer <api_token> on every request to https://connect.mailerlite.com/api.

setupSteps.title: Be aware of rate limits
setupSteps.body: The MailerLite API allows 120 requests per minute (and a lower limit of 5 requests per minute for subscriber import and batch operations). When a limit is exceeded the API returns HTTP 429 with a Retry-After header. An invalid or missing token returns HTTP 401 Unauthenticated.

sources.title: MailerLite Developers - Getting started (conventions, authentication, errors, rate limits)
MailerLite Help - Where to find the MailerLite API key, Group ID and documentation
